message:
fix(tui,serve,palette): eliminate stale state across dashboard panels, harden sidebar focus model, and fix memory daemon lifecycle

## Dashboard Stale-State Elimination

Every dashboard data source (status, health, metrics, history, files, CASS,
Ollama PS, DCG, pending rotations, Agent Mail) previously preserved the last
successful snapshot when a refresh returned an error. This meant the UI would
silently display minutes- or hours-old data with no indication that the live
feed had failed. This commit makes error paths authoritative:

- StatusUpdateMsg errors now clear all pane live-status fields (state, token
  velocity, local perf snapshots) and the agentStatuses map, so stale activity
  badges and output lines cannot persist after the detector goes away.
- Successful StatusUpdateMsg now pre-clears all pane live-status before applying
  fresh results, so panes missing from the latest response lose their stale
  snapshots rather than keeping zombie badges.
- HealthUpdateMsg errors clear all pane health details (status, issues, restart
  count, uptime). Successful health refreshes also pre-clear before applying, so
  panes not in the response lose stale health state.
- MetricsUpdateMsg errors clear metricsData to zero-value instead of leaving the
  last successful coverage/conflict/redundancy/velocity snapshot visible.
- HistoryUpdateMsg errors clear cmdHistory, preventing stale replayable entries
  from remaining actionable in the sidebar.
- FileChangeMsg errors clear fileChanges, preventing stale file-change entries
  from appearing in the sidebar.
- CASSContextMsg errors clear cassContext and propagate to the CASS panel via
  SetData, so stale search hits cannot linger.
- OllamaPSResultMsg errors clear the ollama memory cache and all derived pane
  memory annotations via a new applyOllamaMemorySnapshot helper.
- PendingRotationsUpdateMsg errors clear pending rotations and propagate the
  error to the rotation confirm panel, which now also clears its pending state.
- PTHealthStatesMsg with nil States now clears healthStates (previously the nil
  case was silently ignored, leaving stale PT classification visible).
- DCGUpdateMsg errors now clear dcgAvailable/dcgVersion/dcgBlocked/dcgLastBlocked
  and record dcgError, which renderDCGBadge now displays as a yellow warning.
- AgentMailStatusMsg for disconnected/unavailable state now zeroes lock counts
  and lock info rather than leaving the previous connected-state values visible.
- AgentMailInboxMsg now pre-clears per-pane MailUnread/MailUrgent before applying
  fresh results, so panes missing from the response lose stale unread badges.

New helpers clearPaneLiveStatus, clearPaneHealthDetails, clearAllPaneLiveStatus,
clearAllPaneHealthDetails, and applyOllamaMemorySnapshot encapsulate the
field-level clearing to keep the Update switch cases readable.

## Sidebar Focus Model Refactor

The sidebar previously used IsFocused() calls on individual sub-panels in a
hardcoded if/else chain to decide which panel owned keyboard input. This broke
when new panels were added, required careful ordering, and made it impossible
to cycle focus among sidebar panels.

This commit introduces a sidebarActivePanel field and a formal
sidebarInteractivePanels() registry that returns only panels with visible data
or errors. The new model:

- sidebarActivePanelID() resolves the active panel, defaulting to the first
  interactive panel when the stored value becomes stale.
- cycleSidebarActivePanel(dir) implements J/K cycling among interactive panels.
- activateSidebarPanelForKey(msg) auto-switches focus when a keypress matches
  exactly one non-active panel's keybindings (e.g., pressing 'c' for coverage
  toggle activates the metrics panel even if timeline was previously active).
- syncSidebarPanelFocusState() blurs all sidebar panels then focuses only the
  active one, replacing the scattered Focus/Blur calls in renderSidebar.
- The renderSidebar function now queries sidebarActivePanelID() once and passes
  it to each panel section, replacing per-panel if/else Focus/Blur blocks.
- Key dispatch in the PanelSidebar case of Update now goes through a single
  sidebarActivePanelRef() call instead of the cascading if/else chain.
- The rotation confirm panel is now rendered in the sidebar when it has pending
  rotations or errors, and receives keyboard input (r/c/i/p) when it is the
  active sidebar panel.
- Metrics panel shortcuts (c/r/v/x) are intercepted before the general sidebar
  dispatch when metrics is the active panel.
- Conflicts panel shortcuts (1/2/3) are intercepted when conflicts is focused.
- A new "J/K cycle section" hint is shown in the focused-panel help bar when
  the sidebar has multiple interactive panels.
- getFocusedPanelHints() for PanelSidebar now uses sidebarActivePanelRef()
  instead of the IsFocused() if/else chain.

## Panel Selection Stability

Every panel that displays a list of items now preserves the user's logical
selection across data refreshes, instead of clamping the cursor index and
potentially jumping to a different item when items are inserted/removed/
reordered:

- **Attention panel**: SetData now remembers the selected item's Cursor field
  and restores it after re-sorting. Falls back to SelectNearestCursor when the
  exact item is removed.
- **CASS panel**: SetData generates a composite key (sessionID + sourcePath +
  lineNumber + title + matchType + agent + createdAt) and restores selection by
  key after score-based re-sort. SetData with an error now clears hits.
- **Conflicts panel**: SetConflicts and RemoveConflict use a path+requestor key
  to re-find the selected conflict after list mutations. When the selected
  conflict disappears, selectedAction resets to 0 to prevent stale action state.
- **Files panel**: SetData and time-window cycling use a composite key
  (timestamp + session + path + type + agents) to restore selection. New
  refreshVisibleChanges() helper deduplicates this logic.
- **History panel**: applyFilters now remembers the selected entry ID and
  restores cursor position by ID after filtering. Table row sync is also
  triggered in previously missing code paths.
- **Rotation confirm panel**: SetData remembers the selected AgentID and
  restores it by scanning the new pending list. Errors now clear pending data.
- **Timeline panel**: SetData preserves both the selected agent (by agentID in
  the sorted agent list) and the selected marker (by marker ID, including
  overlay open state). New restoreCursorByAgentID, selectedMarkerState,
  markerByID, and restoreMarkerSelection helpers handle the restoration.

## Keybinding Cleanup

Several advertised keybindings were dead (no handler existed) or conflicted
with other bindings:

- Removed dead 's' (send) binding from keymap and help bar. It was advertised
  in FullHelp() and the help bar hints but had no handler.
- Removed dead left/right/h/l global navigation bindings. They were defined in
  the keymap struct and advertised in FullHelp() but never handled.
- Removed duplicate tab/shift+tab entries from FullHelp() (they appeared in
  both the navigation and panels groups).
- Alerts panel: replaced phantom dismiss/ack_all keybindings with
  pgup/pgdn/home/end scroll navigation (the dismiss/ack_all actions were
  advertised but had no backing handler code).
- Beads panel: replaced phantom claim/open/new keybindings with
  pgup/pgdn/home/end scroll navigation (same dead-binding issue).
- Timeline panel: changed marker navigation from tab/shift+tab to ]/[ to avoid
  conflict with the global panel-cycling tab binding. Changed 'n' (jump to now)
  to '0' and 'm' (first marker) to 'f' to avoid conflicts with other bindings.
- CASS panel: changed search binding from '/' to ctrl+s to avoid conflict with
  the bubbletea list filter.
- Files panel: changed time-window cycle from 'tab' to 't', added 'tab' as
  alternate trigger, and added enter-to-open-file via new OpenFileMsg.
- Metrics panel: keybinding tests now verify the actual key strings match the
  documented bindings (c/r/v/x).

## Open File in Editor

Added the ability to open files from the files panel in the user's $EDITOR:

- New OpenFileMsg in panels/files.go signals the dashboard when enter is pressed
  on a selected file.
- New openFileInEditor command in commands.go resolves relative paths against
  projectDir, validates the file exists and is not a directory, rejects deleted
  files, sanitizes $EDITOR tokens, and launches via tea.ExecProcess.
- New FileOpenResultMsg in messages.go reports editor exit errors to the
  healthMessage status line.
- dashboardBuildEditorCommand and dashboardExecProcess are package-level vars
  for test injection.

## Agent Mail Inbox Body Caching

Agent Mail inbox detail fetches (per-pane message body lookups) previously
used the same generation counter as the inbox summary refresh, causing the
detail response to be silently dropped whenever a summary refresh raced ahead:

- Added refreshAgentMailInboxDetail as a separate generation-counter domain.
- Added agentMailInboxBodyCache and agentMailInboxBodyKnown maps to remember
  previously fetched message bodies across summary refreshes.
- New hydrateInboxBodies, mergeInboxBodies, rememberInboxBodies, and
  cloneInboxMessages helpers manage the body cache lifecycle.
- Summary refreshes now hydrate inbox messages from the body cache before
  storing, so bodies are not lost when a summary refresh arrives.
- Detail refreshes merge into the existing summary instead of replacing it,
  preserving the unread/urgent badge counts.
- New selectedPaneInboxDetailCmd() triggers a detail fetch when the selected
  pane has messages with unknown bodies, wired into cursor movement, number
  quick-select, mouse wheel, mouse click, and pane list key handling.
- AgentMailInboxDetailMsg now has a Skipped field for cases where the fetch
  was intentionally not attempted (no project key, no client, no registry).
- New showInboxDetails toggle (bound to existing InboxToggle key) controls
  whether message body previews appear in the pane detail view.
- renderInboxBodyPreview word-wraps and truncates body text to 3 lines.
- Inbox detail errors are shown inline in the pane detail view.

## Palette Reload Safety

When the palette receives a ReloadMsg (config file changed), the selected
command pointer could become a dangling reference into the old commands slice:

- New reconcileSelectionAfterReload() scans the reloaded commands by key to
  re-anchor the selected pointer, clearing selection and resetting phase if the
  command was removed. XF search results are excluded from reconciliation
  because they are ephemeral selections not backed by palette config.

## Memory Daemon Lifecycle Fix

The memory daemon (cm serve) was started with context.WithTimeout, which killed
the daemon process as soon as the 30-second startup helper returned. The daemon
is meant to be long-lived:

- Replaced CommandContext with plain exec.Command via a package-level
  memoryDaemonCommand function var (testable).
- Added limitedLogBuffer (thread-safe, capped at 8KB) for daemon stderr instead
  of unbounded bytes.Buffer.
- memoryDaemonStartupDelay is now a package-level var for test injection.
- New test TestStartMemoryDaemonAsync_KeepsDaemonAliveAfterStartup verifies
  the helper process PID is still alive after the startup function returns.

## Test Hardening

- process/liveness_test: relaxed GetProcessState assertion to accept any
  non-terminal state (not just R/S), because under load Linux can report D
  (disk sleep) which caused flaky failures.
- session/capture_test: increased per-command budget from 100ms to 150ms,
  reduced sleep from 0.08s to 0.07s, and added elapsed-time assertion to prove
  commands get independent timeout budgets.
- File change producer (fetchFileChangesCmd) now returns the full bounded change
  buffer via tracker.RecordedChanges() instead of hard-capping at 5 minutes,
  because the files panel owns time-window filtering.
- Dashboard pause key (p) now toggles refreshPaused and triggers an immediate
  full refresh on resume, with test coverage.

## Beads Tracker

- Synced issues.jsonl with latest issue state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -98,11 +98,13 @@ func TestGetGitInfo_NonExistentDir(t *testing.T) {
 }
 
 func TestGetGitInfoWithTimeout_UsesIndependentCommandBudgets(t *testing.T) {
+	const perCommandBudget = 150 * time.Millisecond
+
 	tmpBin := t.TempDir()
 	logFile := filepath.Join(tmpBin, "git-invocations.log")
 	gitPath := filepath.Join(tmpBin, "git")
 	script := `#!/bin/sh
-sleep 0.08
+sleep 0.07
 printf '%s\n' "$3 $4 $5" >> "$NTM_GITINFO_LOG"
 case "$3 $4 $5" in
   "rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD")
@@ -123,7 +125,11 @@ esac
 	t.Setenv("PATH", tmpBin+string(os.PathListSeparator)+os.Getenv("PATH"))
 	t.Setenv("NTM_GITINFO_LOG", logFile)
 
-	branch, remote, commit := getGitInfoWithTimeout(t.TempDir(), 100*time.Millisecond)
+	start := time.Now()
+	branch, remote, commit := getGitInfoWithTimeout(t.TempDir(), perCommandBudget)
+	if elapsed := time.Since(start); elapsed < perCommandBudget {
+		t.Fatalf("total runtime = %v, want > %v to prove commands did not share one timeout budget", elapsed, perCommandBudget)
+	}
 	if branch != "main" {
 		t.Fatalf("branch = %q, want main", branch)
 	}

-		{"not enabled", false, false, 0, true, ""},
-		{"enabled not available", true, false, 0, false, "DCG missing"},
-		{"enabled available no blocks", true, true, 0, false, "DCG"},
-		{"enabled available with blocks", true, true, 3, false, "DCG 3 blocked"},
+		{"not enabled", false, false, 0, nil, true, ""},
+		{"enabled error", true, true, 3, errors.New("dcg failed"), false, "DCG error"},
+		{"enabled not available", true, false, 0, nil, false, "DCG missing"},
+		{"enabled available no blocks", true, true, 0, nil, false, "DCG"},
+		{"enabled available with blocks", true, true, 3, nil, false, "DCG 3 blocked"},
 	}
 
 	for _, tc := range tests {

 			got := m.renderDCGBadge()
 			if tc.wantEmpty && got != "" {

-		{k.Tab, k.ShiftTab, k.NextPanel, k.ViewToggle, k.Send},                                  // Panels & Actions
